extraction.one_line_summary: JACOBSEN Fabio won his fourth sprint with overwhelming speed advantage, needing only 100 meters of acceleration to distance the field.
tactical_patterns: Lotto and Euskaltel launched serious counter-attack on Alto de Ijas climb to bridge, forcing peloton tempo control decisions.
VAN HOEK Harm bridged solo with fresh legs after breakaway fatigued, reducing 28 seconds in final kilometer descent.
Sprint teams deliberately throttled peloton pace mid-race to avoid redlining sprinters on prolonged 180km flat stage.
Alpecin-Deceuninck positioned perfectly on front at final kilometer but JACOBSEN required only two pedal strokes to gap entire field.

rider_signals.JACOBSEN Fabio: positive: Speed differential so extreme he sprinted 100m maximum while TRENTIN led early but faded, suggesting explosive power reserve management
rider_signals.TRENTIN Matteo: negative: Jumped earliest but immediately gapped by JACOBSEN's first acceleration, revealing top-end speed deficit despite positioning
rider_signals.EVENEPOEL Remco: neutral: Commentary suggests potential mathematical challenge to green jersey via time trial and mountaintop finishes, signaling confidence
rider_signals.SIMMONS Quinn: neutral: Breakaway rider expected to respond to radio information about counter-attacks but failed to increase tempo on Alto de Ijas
